A very long rod 5 mm diameter has one end maintained at 100°C. The surface of the rod is exposed to ambient air at 25°C with a convection heat trasnfer coefficient of 100 W/m2K. What will be the heat loss from the rod? [Assume thermal conductivity, k = 398 W/mK]

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

temperature gradient at the surface in the solid is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about fins are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)
A fin made of high thermal conductivity material is more efficient as it maintains a more uniform
temperature along it’s length.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A thermocouple is the form of a long cylinder of 2 mm diameter initially at 30°C is used to measure the temperature of a cold gas at –160°C. The convection coefficient over the surface is 60 W/m2K. The material properties are : density = 8922 kg/m3; specific heat = 410 J/kgK, conductivity = 22.7 W/mK. The time it will take to indicate –150°C is _____ sec. [Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 89.76
Solution

89.76 (88 - 92)

Solution

4 (4 - 4)

Q9 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

Two perpendicular planes of each 2 m × 2 m meet at a common edge. The horizontal plane has an emissivity of 0.4 and the vertical plane has an emissivity of 0.6. If these planes are at 400 K and 600 K respectively, then the net radiation exchange is ______ kW [Rounds upto two decimal
places] [Take shape factor from the horizontal to the vertical plane of 0.20004]

NAT
Correct answer: 3.29
Solution

3.29 (3.20 - 3.40)

 
Q10 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

On clear nights there is radiation from Earths surface to the space. On such a night, the water particles on plant leaves radiate to the sky whose temperature may be taken as –70°C. The water particles receives heat from air surrounding it by convection, the convection heat transfer coefficient having a value of 28 W/m2K. If the water should not freeze, then the temperature of surrounding air will be _____°C. [Assuming water surface to be black] [Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 7.81
Solution

7.81 (7.70 - 7.90)

Q17 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A composite wall separates combustion gases at 2600°C from liquid at 100°C, with gas and liquid side heat transfer coefficients of 50 and 1000 W/m2K respectively. The wall is composed of a 10 mm thick layer of beryllium oxide (k = 21.5 W/mK) on the gas side and 20 mm thick slab of stainless steel (k = 25.4 W/mK) on the liquid side. The contact resistance between the oxide and the steel is 0.05 m2K/W. For 1-D steady state heat conduction, the rate of heat loss per unit surface area is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q18 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A steel strap is serving as a support for the steam pipe shown in the adjoining figure. The strap is welded to the pipe and bolted to the ceiling the junction between the support strut and the ceiling is adiabatic, and the outside temperature of the steam pipe is 110°C. The strut is 80 cm
high, 12.5 cm wide and 0.3 cm thick. The rate at which heat is lost to the surrounding air by the support strut is ________ W. [Correct upto two decimal places]. It may be assumed that thermal conductivity for steel is 45 W/mK, the total outside surface coefficient is 17 W/m2K and the surrounding air is at 32°C.

 
NAT
Correct answer: 21.14
Solution

21.14 (21.05 - 21.25)

 
Q19 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a parallel flow heat exchanger, water at a rate of 5400 kg/hr enters at 20°C to cool oil at a rate of 7200 kg/hr entering at 120°C. The overall heat transfer coefficient is 200 W/m2K, and heat exchanger area is 25 m2. Considering the specific heats of water and oil as 4.2 kJ/kgK and 2 kJ/
kgK respectively. The temperature of water at exit is:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

and heat transfer coefficient of 40.7 W/m2.K, the length of shaft necessary between the motor and pump is [Assuming, no heat loss from the fin tip]

 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Which of the following statement is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)

resistance to heat flow, the overall heat transfer coefficient for the heat exchanger is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3 (2.7 - 3.3)

Correct answer: B

(b)

_____ W/m2K. [Correct upto two decimal places]

Correct answer: 46.76
Solution

A long thin glass walled, 0.3 cm diameter, mercury thermometer is placed in a stream of air with convection coefficient of 60 W/m2K for measuring transient temperature of air. Consider cylindrical thermometer bulb consists of mercury only. For which k = 8.9 W/mK and α = 0.016 m2/h.
Time required for the temperature change to reach half of its initial value is _____sec. [Correct upto two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 17.33
Solution
